title: iliamna bakeri
intro: Iliamna bakeri, also known as Baker's Woolly-heads, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Thelypodiaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting tissue repair, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. The herbal preparations of Iliamna bakeri involve infusing the plant in water or oil to create topical treatments, such as ointments or salves, which can be applied directly to affected areas.
